_comment: Curated seed for harvest.R. Packages are auto-read from local DESCRIPTION; this file supplies canonical doc-site URLs, CRAN flags, owners, tags, the tutorials list, and the lamethods book volumes. verify_links.R validates every URL live, so any drift here is caught at build time.

about.title: Dynalytics
about.tagline: A framework for the rigorous analytics of dynamical systems.
about.lead: Dynalytics is an overarching framework and methodological ecosystem for the analysis and rigorous validation of the dynamics of dynamical systems. It encompasses a diverse family of models — transition networks, co-occurrence networks, psychological networks, and higher-order networks — unified by a single philosophy of scientific rigour. The R, Python, and JavaScript libraries catalogued here are its software layer.
about.more: What sets Dynalytics apart is that analysis and validation are inseparable. A multi-level confirmatory testing battery validates every supported model and every analytical claim: split-half reliability for internal consistency, bootstrapping for edge-level stability, case-dropping for centrality stability, and permutation-based comparisons for groups, conditions, and temporal phases. Where most methods stop at describing a pattern, Dynalytics asks whether that pattern would survive rigorous statistical scrutiny — and supplies the tools to find out.
about.closing: The conceptual groundwork applies to any network or process derived from observed interactions, sequences, or co-occurrence: social, behavioural, cognitive. Its value is not a single technique but a unified theoretical and methodological language for describing change and stability in any dynamic system.
about.peopleIntro: The researchers who build and maintain the Dynalytics toolkit across University of Eastern Finland, FernUniversität in Hagen, and University of Melbourne.

newsletter.heading: Get Dynalytics updates
newsletter.blurb: New packages, releases, papers, and tutorials — occasional, no spam.
newsletter.note: We'll only email about the ecosystem catalogued here. Unsubscribe anytime.
newsletter.success: Thanks — please check your inbox to confirm your subscription.

points.t: Every analytical claim must be matched by evidence appropriate to the structure, scope, and complexity of the inference being made — the complexity of the evidence must match the complexity of the claim.

Transition networks (TNA, FTNA, ATNA), co-occurrence networks (CNA, WTNA), heterogeneous (HTNA) and multilayer (MCML) networks, psychological networks, and higher-order networks (HON) — each named for the structure it estimates.

Split-half reliability for internal consistency, bootstrapping for edge stability, case-dropping for centrality stability, and permutation tests for group comparison — so findings reflect genuine system behaviour, not noise or chance.
Implemented across open-source R, Python, and JavaScript libraries plus web and desktop applications — the packages indexed below.

papers.title: Dynalytics: The Birth of a Framework for Rigorous Analytics of Dynamical Systems
papers.blurb: Introduces the Dynalytics framework — a family of models, a multi-level confirmatory testing battery, and the scientific-contract philosophy that ties the ecosystem together.

papers.title: Transition Network Analysis: A Novel Framework for Modeling, Visualizing, and Identifying the Temporal Patterns of Learners and Learning Processes
papers.authors: Saqr, López-Pernas, Törmänen, Kaliisa, Misiejuk & Tikka
papers.blurb: The canonical, foundational TNA paper — introduces the framework for modelling, visualising, and identifying the temporal patterns of learners and learning processes.

papers.title: tna: An R Package for Transition Network Analysis
papers.blurb: The official software paper for the tna R package — cite this when you use the package.

papers.title: Capturing the Process of Students' AI Interactions When Creating and Learning Complex Network Structures
papers.blurb: Captures the process of students' AI interactions while creating and learning complex network structures.

papers.title: The Dynamics of the Self-Regulation Process in Student-AI Interactions: The Case of Problem-Solving in Programming Education
papers.blurb: Models the dynamics of self-regulation in student–AI interactions during programming problem-solving.

papers.title: Cognitive Offloading in Student–AI Collaboration: A Longitudinal Analysis of Prompting Strategies
papers.blurb: A longitudinal analysis of prompting strategies and cognitive offloading in student–AI collaboration.

papers.title: Frequency Transition Network Analysis (FTNA)
papers.authors: Saqr, Misiejuk, Törmänen, Kaliisa, Tikka & López-Pernas
papers.blurb: Introduces Frequency Transition Network Analysis (FTNA) — modelling observed transition frequencies directly, without Markov assumptions.

papers.title: JTNA: A Desktop Software for Transition Network Analysis
papers.blurb: A point-and-click desktop (jamovi) software bringing Transition Network Analysis to non-programmers.

papers.title: Expanding the Quantitative Ethnography Toolkit with Transition Network Analysis: Exploring Methodological Synergies and Boundaries
papers.blurb: Brings Transition Network Analysis into the Quantitative Ethnography toolkit — methodological synergies and boundaries.

papers.title: Role Dynamics in Student-AI Collaboration: A Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis Approach
papers.blurb: Uses heterogeneous TNA to model role dynamics in student–AI collaboration.

papers.title: Modeling the Dynamics and Impact of Human-AI Interactions with Attention Transition Network Analysis
papers.blurb: Applies Attention Transition Network Analysis (ATNA) to the dynamics and impact of human–AI interactions.

papers.title: Human-AI Collaboration or Obedient and Often Clueless AI in Instruct, Serve, Repeat Dynamics?
papers.blurb: A critical examination of human–AI collaboration and the 'instruct, serve, repeat' dynamic.

papers.title: How AI-Generated Feedback Hinders or Helps Learning: A Heterogeneous TNA Study of Learning Dynamics
papers.blurb: A heterogeneous TNA of how first-grade students engage with real-time AI feedback on multimodal mathematics tasks — when it supports learning and when it gets in the way.

papers.title: Unpacking Vibe Coding: Help-Seeking Processes in Student–AI Interactions While Programming
papers.authors: Rinja, Oliveira, López-Pernas, Saqr, Specht & Misiejuk
papers.blurb: A Heterogeneous TNA of 19,418 interaction turns from 110 undergraduates, framing AI-assisted 'vibe coding' as help-seeking — top performers pursue instrumental inquiry that elicits tutor-like responses, while low performers fall into more passive patterns.

news.title: lagdynamics: modern lag sequential analysis
news.blurb: A tidy, pipe-friendly toolkit for lag sequential analysis of categorical event sequences. A single lsa() constructor fits classical, two-cell, bidirectional, and dominance engines; every result reads through a tidy verb; and a full confirmatory battery — bootstrap, Dirichlet–Multinomial certainty, split-half reliability, case-drop stability, permutation and Bayesian group comparison — quantifies the evidence behind each edge.

news.title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis in R
news.blurb: The R package behind the Heterogeneous TNA papers. It models sequences that mix two or more actor groups (e.g. human and AI) within one interaction — a thin API over the Nestimate and cograph engines builds a transition network across the combined sequence while preserving the actor partition, so bootstrap, permutation, reliability, centrality and plotting treat each actor's codes as a distinct node group.

news.title: Nestimate: one engine for dynamic & psychological networks
news.blurb: An R package to estimate, compare, and analyse dynamic and psychological networks through a unified interface — transition, frequency, co-occurrence and attention-weighted TNA estimation, psychometric methods (correlation, partial correlation, graphical lasso, Ising), and higher-order network methods — with bootstrap inference, permutation testing, split-half reliability, and centrality-stability analysis.

news.title: snakeplot: serpentine plots for long timelines
news.blurb: A new R package for visualising long timelines, extended sequences, and temporally chained survey or experience-sampling data as intuitive serpentine ('snake') plots — with distribution bars, tick-mark plots, inter-item correlation arcs, faceted multi-construct panels, and time-of-day positioning for ecological momentary assessment data.

news.title: Saqrmisc: miscellaneous tools for data analysis
news.blurb: A new R package of miscellaneous functions for data processing, statistical analysis, and visualisation — including model-based clustering with MoEClust, comparison plots with built-in statistical testing, and mosaic-plot analysis for categorical variables, wrapped in robust error handling and a friendly interface.

news.title: Heterogeneous TNA: how AI feedback helps or hinders learning
news.blurb: Out now in the Journal of Computer Assisted Learning. López-Pernas, Misiejuk & Saqr use Heterogeneous TNA to trace how first-grade students engage with real-time AI feedback while solving multimodal mathematics tasks — showing, process by process, when the feedback supports learning and when it gets in the way.

news.title: Heterogeneous TNA: unpacking ‘vibe coding’ in student–AI programming
news.blurb: A new preprint applies Heterogeneous TNA to 19,418 interaction turns from 110 undergraduates, framing AI-assisted ‘vibe coding’ as help-seeking. Top performers engage in instrumental inquiry and exploration that elicits tutor-like AI responses, while low performers fall into more passive patterns — a process-level contrast only the heterogeneous model surfaces.

news.title: transitiontrees is on CRAN
news.blurb: Now available from CRAN, transitiontrees analyses how states follow one another and where pathways branch or converge. It models trajectories with variable-order prediction suffix trees — in both frequency- and prediction-based forms — and visualises them as transition trees, radial sunbursts, heatmaps, and forward-trajectory trees.

news.title: cooccure: co-occurrence networks from any data
news.blurb: A new R package that builds co-occurrence networks from delimited fields, long/bipartite tables, binary matrices, or wide sequences. It returns tidy edge tables with eight similarity measures — including Jaccard, cosine, and association strength — plus scaling, thresholding, splitting, and multi-format export.

news.title: bibnets: bibliometric networks in R
news.blurb: A new R package for importing, constructing, and exporting bibliometric networks — reading Scopus, Web of Science, OpenAlex, Crossref, Lens, Dimensions, BibTeX and RIS exports. It goes beyond standard co-networks with attention-weighted edges, position-aware counting, temporal windows, disparity-filter backbones, and historiographs.

news.title: The Dynalytics framework paper is out
news.blurb: The preprint introducing Dynalytics — its family of models, the confirmatory testing battery, and the scientific-contract philosophy — is now available on SSRN.

news.title: Cognitive offloading in student–AI collaboration
news.blurb: A longitudinal analysis of prompting strategies and cognitive offloading in student–AI collaboration, published in Computers in Human Behavior Reports.

news.title: JTNA brings Transition Network Analysis to jamovi
news.blurb: A point-and-click desktop module puts the full TNA workflow — build, plot, and statistically test transition networks — in the hands of non-programmers.

news.title: CRETIC funded by the Research Council of Finland
news.blurb: A new project on optimising clinical reasoning in time-critical scenarios, using learning-analytics methods from the ecosystem.

news.title: ISILA and IGLUE: two new Erasmus+ projects
news.blurb: Erasmus+ funding for early-intervention learning analytics (ISILA) and game-based escape-room learning (IGLUE).

news.title: Transition Network Analysis introduced at LAK ’25
news.blurb: The foundational TNA paper — a framework for modelling, visualising, and identifying the temporal patterns of learners and learning processes.

tools.title: tnapy — Transition Network Analysis for Python
tools.blurb: A Python implementation of Transition Network Analysis for users working outside the R ecosystem.

tools.title: JTNA — Transition Network Analysis in jamovi
tools.blurb: A point-and-click jamovi module for TNA: build, plot, and statistically test transition networks with no code.

tools.title: Dynalytics — interactive web app
tools.blurb: A browser-based Shiny application for the Dynalytics framework — run the analyses in the browser, no installation required.

tools.title: TNA — interactive web app
tools.blurb: A browser-based Shiny application for Transition Network Analysis.

posts.title: cograph: Complex Network Analysis and Visualization
posts.desc: A modern R package for analysis, visualization and manipulation of complex networks with publication-ready output.

posts.title: Human–AI Interaction: A Transition Network Analysis with cograph
posts.desc: Sequential analysis of 13,002 turns across 429 human–AI coding sessions, mapped with TNA and cograph.

posts.title: JTNA for Transition Network Analysis
posts.desc: Statistical rigour for sequential behavioural analysis — now in jamovi via JTNA.

posts.title: Precision Education is the Answer
posts.desc: Beyond group averages: a case for precision education.

posts.title: Rohy: AI-Powered Virtual Patient Simulator
posts.desc: An open-source medical simulation platform with AI-driven patient conversations.

posts.title: Book: Learning Analytics Methods and Tutorials
posts.desc: Learning Analytics Methods and Tutorials: a practical guide using R.

posts.title: ISILA — Funding from Erasmus+
posts.desc: Early-intervention learning-analytics methods for quality and sustainability.

posts.title: CRETIC — Funding from the Research Council of Finland
posts.desc: Optimising clinical reasoning in time-critical scenarios.

book_volumes.title: Vol 1 — LA Methods & Tutorials
book_volumes.blurb: Learning Analytics Methods and Tutorials: a practical guide using R — data, statistics, visualization, clustering, sequence analysis, and networks.

book_volumes.title: Vol 2 — Advanced LA Methods
book_volumes.blurb: Advanced Learning Analytics Methods: AI and explainable AI, LLMs and NLP, complex systems, and advanced transition network analysis.
